what_it_does: React component that renders a builder chat panel embedding a live Claude Code session: it accepts user prompts, streams tool/text activity from /api/claude/turn, displays an indicated element context chip, lets the user arm a bounded forager selection-set context block, and can hand the last builder description off to a build-intent approval gate.
observations: Starts minimized and can be expanded via the header toggle.
Uses a kit/corpus class vocabulary (.hud, .rhm, .msg, .who, .txt, .rhm-indicating, .b) with no literal colors in code.
Maintains a local message log with roles user, builder, and act.
Sends prompts to /api/claude/turn with session_id, indicated address, and an optional context_block.
Streams newline-delimited JSON events (init, tool, text, error, done) and decodes them with TextDecoder.
Arms a one-shot forager selection-set context block capped at 6KB with per-item head truncated to 400 characters.
Listens for a custom window 'builder-context' event to receive an externally composed context block.
Provides a 'build this' button that turns the last builder message into a build-intent via mintBuildIntent.
Fail-loud: reports non-OK responses, stream breaks, and turns that end without a terminal done/error event.
standouts: Live Claude Code session surfaced as a side-panel with transparent tool-line activity.
Bounded, replace-semantics selection-set block composed from forager multi-select.
Single approved exit point from conversation to consequence through mintBuildIntent.
conventions: React functional component with hooks and refs for local state and DOM scrolling.
Custom window event listener for cross-panel context handoff.
Streaming fetch parsed manually with getReader and TextDecoder.
Design-system class names used instead of inline styles or literal colors.
Comments encode design rationale and safety rules.
concerns: The 'builder-context' event handler types the event as any.
sessionRef updates do not trigger re-renders, so the 'session live' indicator may lag until the next state change.
ctxBlock is cleared immediately after a successful HTTP response, before the stream finishes; a late stream failure would lose the armed block.
The message class mapping assigns act messages the assistant class because the ternary only distinguishes user vs non-user.
The 'build this' button is computed inside an IIFE in the JSX render body.
notes: A self-contained builder chat surface that investigates the repo with pointed and sculpted context, streams its work, and only mutates the system through an external approval gate.
questions: What is the exact return shape and behavior of mintBuildIntent from AppContext?
What object shape does useForagerSelection return and what fields are guaranteed on each item?
How does the backend /api/claude/turn manage session resumption and plan-mode enforcement?
Which component or gesture dispatches the 'builder-context' custom event?
What does indicate(null) do and how is the indicated address established?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file implements the described builder side-panel, uses the shared indicated locus, streams a real Claude Code session, and routes wanted changes through the WireRequest/mintBuildIntent approval gate rather than mutating directly.
apparent_themes: Safe-by-construction: plan-mode default and no direct mutation from the panel.
Human-in-the-loop: every change exits through an approval gate.
Context sculpting: bounded, replace-semantics selection-set blocks.
Streaming transparency: tool lines and text are shown live.
Separation of concerns: contractor panel vs entity chat.
intention_signals: To give the operator a conversational contractor that can inspect the actual codebase at a pointed address and with selected forager context, narrate its investigation live, and convert its description into a reviewed build intent rather than acting unilaterally.
novelty: Embedding a real Claude Code session inside the surface as a transparent, context-aware builder panel with a bounded forager selection-set injection and a single approved handoff to build-intent.
contribution: A builder chat UI that keeps session state locally, streams activity visibly, carries an indicated element address and an armed forager context block into each turn, and exposes one controlled exit to the existing build-intent review flow.
summary_for_embedding: ClaudeChat.tsx is a React builder chat panel that streams a live Claude Code session, displays pointed-element and forager-set context chips, and hands the last builder description to an external build-intent approval gate.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a safe, transparent conversational contractor that investigates the repo with user-provided context and only proposes changes through an approval gate.

symbols.does: Type alias for a panel message with role and text.
symbols.description: PanelMsg defines the local chat log entry shape, distinguishing user messages, builder text responses, and act/tool activity lines.

symbols.does: React component that renders the builder chat panel and manages its streaming session.
symbols.description: ClaudeChat is the exported component that wires together the indicated address, forager selection context, message log, streaming fetch to /api/claude/turn, and the build-intent handoff button.

symbols.does: Event handler for the custom 'builder-context' window event.
symbols.description: h receives an externally dispatched context block, arms it as the next turn's context_block, and expands the minimized panel.

symbols.does: Appends a message to the log and scrolls the log to the bottom.
symbols.description: push updates the msgs state immutably and schedules a microtask to keep the chat log scrolled to the latest entry.

symbols.does: Composes a bounded context block from the current forager selection and arms it for the next turn.
symbols.description: giveToBuilder builds a replace-semantics selection-set block from useForagerSelection items, truncating each head to 400 characters and capping total length at 6KB, then arms it as ctxBlock.

symbols.does: Posts the user prompt to /api/claude/turn and streams the response into the log.
symbols.description: send sends the prompt along with session_id, indicated address, and the armed context_block, then reads newline-delimited JSON events to populate the log with tool, text, error, and done messages.
